rubric_items.reasoning: Agent identified the true root cause (canary-canary Ingress's canary-by-header-value annotation was set to 'wrong' instead of 'always') and fixed exactly that field via a targeted annotate command. This is a durable, root-cause fix — the annotation is persisted server-side and would survive restarts/re-checks. Both required behaviors (with/without header) were verified post-fix and the oracle confirms pass.

The agent read the existing Ingress objects in the namespace first (diagnosis), spotted the misconfigured annotation value among several decoy ingresses (canary-alt, canary-stable, etc.), reasoned explicitly about why the header wasn't matching, then applied the single corrective action and verified with the two prescribed curl tests. No backtracking, no trial-and-error — a coherent diagnose-then-fix-then-verify sequence in just 4 turns.

Used read-only 'kubectl get ingress -o yaml' for diagnosis, then a single scoped 'kubectl annotate ingress canary-canary ... --overwrite' to fix only the specific misconfigured annotation, followed by the exact curl-based verification commands provided in the task. Least-privilege, targeted, no broad or risky operations.

Exactly one mutation (the annotate call) touching only the relevant field on the relevant resource. No Deployments or Services were touched, no unrelated decoy ingresses (canary-alt, canary-stable, demo-app, demo-ingress, legacy-ingress) were modified despite being present and could have caused confusion. Minimal, focused change matching the API activity log (1 mutation, 8 reads).
